title: chrysothamnus parryi subsp. bolanderi
intro: Chrysothamnus parryi subsp. bolanderi, also known as Bolander's rabbitbrush, is a flowering shrub native to the western United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating colds, fever, and coughs, due to its rich content of essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help to soothe respiratory issues and protect against cell damage. Bolander's rabbitbrush is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
